hooks.command: if [ "${DO_NOT_TRACK:-}" != "1" ]; then marker="$HOME/.upfront/.installed"; project_id=$(git remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null | shasum -a 256 | cut -c1-16 || echo 'unknown'); if [ ! -f "$marker" ]; then event='plugin_install'; mkdir -p "$HOME/.upfront" && touch "$marker"; else event='plugin_session'; fi; curl -sf -X POST 'https://www.google-analytics.com/mp/collect?measurement_id=G-EBNJWXFM9Z&api_secret=eVASlA0qTN64HAheMPzPWA' -d '{"client_id":"'"$project_id"'","events":[{"name":"'"$event"'","params":{"version":"0.3.1"}}]}' >/dev/null 2>&1; fi; latest=$(curl -sf --max-time 3 https://api.github.com/repos/ThinkUpfront/Upfront/releases/latest | grep tag_name | cut -d'"' -f4 2>/dev/null) && [ -n "$latest" ] && [ "$latest" != 'v0.3.1' ] && echo "Upfront $latest available (you have 0.3.1). Run: claude plugin marketplace add ThinkUpfront/Upfront" || true

hooks.prompt: The tool call in $ARGUMENTS is about to edit a file. If the file is a linter config, formatter config, type checker config, or CI/CD pipeline file (e.g. .eslintrc, .prettierrc, tsconfig.json, .rubocop.yml, .golangci.yml, pyproject.toml linting sections, .github/workflows/*, Makefile lint targets, pre-commit config, biome.json, deno.json), check: is the edit WEAKENING a rule (disabling, loosening, adding ignores/exceptions)? If yes, respond with exactly: {"decision": "block", "reason": "Fix the code, not the config. Weakening linter/formatter rules hides problems instead of solving them."}. If the edit is ADDING or STRENGTHENING rules, or if the file is not a config file, respond with: {"decision": "allow"}.

The tool call in $ARGUMENTS is about to write a file. If the file is a linter config, formatter config, type checker config, or CI/CD pipeline file (e.g. .eslintrc, .prettierrc, tsconfig.json, .rubocop.yml, .golangci.yml, pyproject.toml linting sections, .github/workflows/*, Makefile lint targets, pre-commit config, biome.json, deno.json), check: is the write REMOVING or WEAKENING existing rules (disabling, loosening, adding ignores/exceptions) compared to what a stricter version would have? If this is a brand new config file being created, allow it. If it's overwriting an existing config to weaken rules, respond with exactly: {"decision": "block", "reason": "Fix the code, not the config. Weakening linter/formatter rules hides problems instead of solving them."}. Otherwise respond with: {"decision": "allow"}.

hooks.command: if [ "${DO_NOT_TRACK:-}" != "1" ]; then skill_name=$(cat /dev/stdin | grep -o '"skill_name":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4 2>/dev/null || echo 'unknown'); project_id=$(git remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null | shasum -a 256 | cut -c1-16 || echo 'unknown'); curl -sf -X POST 'https://www.google-analytics.com/mp/collect?measurement_id=G-EBNJWXFM9Z&api_secret=eVASlA0qTN64HAheMPzPWA' -d '{"client_id":"'"$project_id"'","events":[{"name":"skill_used","params":{"skill_name":"'"$skill_name"'","version":"0.3.1"}}]}' >/dev/null 2>&1; fi
